steps.title: Set up the unit conversion
steps.content: This is a rate conversion. Convert square centimeters to square meters using $1\text{ m}^2=10{,}000\text{ cm}^2$, then convert hours to minutes using $1\text{ hour}=60\text{ minutes}$.

steps.title: Identify conversion factors for area and time
steps.content:
To convert 240 cm²/hour to m²/minute, we need two conversions:
  • Area: 1 m = 100 cm, so 1 m² = 100² = 10,000 cm²
  • Time: 1 hour = 60 minutes

steps.title: Apply the conversions
steps.content:
Multiply the given rate by both conversion factors: $$240 \frac{\text{cm}^2}{\text{hour}} \times \frac{1 \text{ m}^2}{10,000 \text{ cm}^2} \times \frac{1 \text{ hour}}{60 \text{ min}} = \frac{240}{10,000 \times 60}$$
Simplify the fraction: $$\frac{240}{600,000} = \frac{4}{10,000} = 0.0004 \text{ m}^2/\text{min}$$
